data.current_description:
Remove the records provided from the list. Records that do not exist or that are not members of the list are ignored.

This endpoint only works for lists that have a `processingType` of `MANUAL` or `SNAPSHOT`.
data.description_suggestion: Remove specified records from a list. Accepts a collection of record IDs in the request body to be removed from the list. Records that do not exist or are not members of the list are silently ignored. This endpoint only works for lists with `processingType` of `MANUAL` or `SNAPSHOT`. Returns a 200 response with details about the removal operation, including the number of records successfully removed.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks clarity about input format (request body structure) and output format (response content). Added explicit details about expected input (collection of record IDs), response status code, and response content to help AI agents understand what data to provide and what to expect in return.

data.current_summary: Translate Legacy List Id to Modern List Id
data.current_description: This API allows translation of legacy list id to list id. This is a temporary API allowed for mapping old id's to new id's and will expire on May 30th, 2025.
data.description_suggestion: Translate a single legacy list ID to its corresponding modern list ID. Accepts a legacy list ID as input and returns the mapped modern list ID. This is a temporary migration API that will expire on May 30th, 2025. Use for systems transitioning from legacy to modern list ID formats.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about input/output format and practical use case. Added specificity about what parameter is expected, what is returned, and context about the migration purpose to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Translate Legacy List Id to Modern List Id in Batch
data.current_description: This API allows translation of a batch of legacy list id's to list id's. This allows for a maximum of 10,000 id's. This is a temporary API allowed for mapping old id's to new id's and will expire on May 30th, 2025.
data.description_suggestion: Translate multiple legacy list IDs to their corresponding modern list IDs in a single batch request. Accepts an array of legacy list IDs (maximum 10,000 IDs per request) and returns a mapping of legacy IDs to modern IDs. This is a temporary migration API that will expire on May 30th, 2025. Use for bulk migration of list ID references.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about input structure (array format) and output format (mapping structure). Added specificity about request/response format, batch size constraint, and practical use case for bulk migrations to improve AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Retrieves a folder and recursively includes all folders via the childNodes attribute.  The child lists field will be empty in all child nodes. Only the folder retrieved will include the child lists in that folder.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve folder hierarchy with nested folders and lists
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the folder hierarchy starting from the specified folder, recursively including all child folders via the childNodes attribute. Child folders will have empty child lists fields, while only the root folder will include its direct child lists. This operation provides a complete view of the folder structure and list organization.
data.reasoning: Summary says 'a folder' (singular) but operation retrieves hierarchical structure. Description is confusing with inconsistent terminology ('childNodes' vs 'child lists'). Improved summary clarifies the hierarchical nature, and description better explains the recursive structure and data population rules for AI agent clarity.
